governance.templateInstructions: This repository is the canonical source for the TCTBP-Web templates and runner architecture. Replace repo-specific values in downstream projects. Scaffolded projects receive a populated non-template profile.

project.name: TCTBP-Web
project.description: Canonical template repository for web/browser-based projects using the TCTBP workflow with deterministic Node.js runners, staged branch model, code-loss prevention, and a project scaffold factory.

simple.description: Single production branch only. Suitable for template repos, libraries, and non-deployed projects.

staged.description: Working → staging → production promotion chain. Suitable for deployed web applications.

promotionPolicy.workingToStaging: Use promote staging to merge development into staging before deploying the staging environment.
promotionPolicy.stagingToProduction: Use promote production to merge staging into main before shipping and deploying production.

branchModel.comment: TCTBP-Web itself uses 'simple' because it ships a template, not a runtime. Scaffolded projects default to 'staged'.

verification.comment: Blocking verification for promote, deploy, and release workflows uses test. Lint and build are added as blocking gates when the project configures them.

devServer.comment: Set devServer.port and devServer.label when the project adds a dev server (e.g. Vite on 5173). The runtime advisory in handover will check this port.

scaffold.purpose: Create a new web project with the full TCTBP-Web runtime surface pre-installed and configured from interactive answers. The new project opens on the working branch ready for development.
scaffold.comment: Scaffold asks 6 questions (project name, target path, working branch, branch strategy, deploy target, test framework), creates the project from scratch, runs git init, writes the full TCTBP-Web runtime surface, generates a populated TCTBP.json profile, creates the branch structure, opens on the working branch, and smoke-tests the installed runners.

ship.purpose: Create a formal shipped production version from a clean, fetched main branch while keeping version files, release notes, tags, and remote state recoverable and unambiguous.
ship.branchPolicyComment: Reserve SHIP for main so tags remain production release markers.

publish.purpose: Safely publish the current clean branch to origin, including first publication when no upstream exists yet, without bumping version, creating a tag, or changing deployment state.

checkpoint.purpose: Create a durable local-only checkpoint commit on the current branch so work is preserved without version, tag, metadata, or remote side effects.

handover.purpose: Safely checkpoint and publish the active branch so development can continue on another machine without promoting code between environment branches.

localOnlyVariant.purpose: Create a local-only handover checkpoint without publishing to origin.

runtimeAdvisory.comment: If the configured dev server port is active, report it as operator advice only. Never stop processes automatically.

handover.comment: Handover is a sync workflow, not a promotion workflow. It publishes the current branch only and must not stop local runtime processes automatically.

resume.purpose: Safely fast-forward local branches that are behind their origin counterpart after switching machines or returning to active development. Diverged branches are always skipped. The working tree must be clean before resuming.
resume.comment: Resume is a read-and-sync workflow only. It never rewrites history, never force-pushes, and never switches branches. Diverged branches are reported but never mutated.

abort.purpose: Inspect partial workflow state and propose deterministic recovery actions before executing any local abort operation. By default this runner is preview-only and requires --apply for execution.
abort.comment: Abort executes only local git abort commands when explicitly approved. It never force-pushes, never rewrites history, and does not mutate remote state.

gate.purpose: Expose explicit trigger phrases for test, lint, and build quality gates through a single runner.
gate.comment: Gates report 'not configured' gracefully when the corresponding command is null in the profile.

version.purpose: Report branch/version/tag alignment using a consistent TCTBP runner entrypoint.
version.comment: Use 'version status' for informational output and 'version check' when strict mismatch exit semantics are required.

rollback.purpose: Safely revert the latest checkpoint commit while preserving history. Preview by default, execute only with explicit --apply.
rollback.comment: Rollback is intended for undoing checkpoint commits only and uses git revert (never reset/rewrite history).

statusReport.comment: For status, the first user-visible output block must be the fuller local-versus-origin comparison table using exactly the configured columns `Origin`, `Local`, `Status`, and `Action(s)`. Emit it as a standalone Markdown table block with a blank line before and after the table. Do not replace the table with prose, do not place prose on the same line as the table header, and do not emit a status-only reply without the table. Include the current branch, default branch, tags, ahead/behind state, and handover relevance. Use 'n/a' where no meaningful origin-side value exists.

handoverReport.comment: At the end of handover, render a concise summary table that confirms the active branch, current head commit, tag state, current-branch publication state, and clean synced baseline. Emit it as a standalone Markdown table block with a blank line before and after the table. Keep it shorter than status and focused on the handover result.

checkpointReport.comment: At the end of checkpoint, render a concise summary table focused on the actual commit transition and the resulting local-only baseline. Emit it as a standalone Markdown table block with a blank line before and after the table. Include the previous HEAD commit, the new checkpoint commit, the resulting working-tree state, the branch sync state, and an explicit confirmation that remote state did not change. Use 'n/a' where no meaningful origin-side value exists.

statusRecommendations.priorityNote: Array is ordered by priority: recover partial state first, then resume/sync, then local preservation, then publish, then ship, then handover.

rules.resume: Suggest when the active work branch or default branch does not match origin, or when resuming on another machine would require a clean fast-forward.
rules.checkpoint: Suggest when the current branch has non-ignored uncommitted work that should be preserved locally before publish, handover, ship, or branch-transition work continues.
rules.publish: Suggest when the current branch is clean, ahead of origin or missing an upstream, and the user likely wants to sync the branch without release or handover side effects.
rules.handover: Suggest when the active branch is cleanly ahead of origin or the working tree has local changes that need a durable multi-machine checkpoint.
rules.ship: Suggest when there are unshipped commits since the last reachable vX.Y.Z tag, version/tag drift exists, or release notes are missing for a user-visible shipped change.
rules.abort: Suggest when a partial workflow state is detected, such as merge in progress, bump/tag mismatch, unpublished release tag, or failed prior workflow.
rules.none: Suggest when the branch is clean, synced, and no SHIP or handover is needed.

branch.purpose: Safely close out a short-lived working branch into the default branch, then either stop on the default branch or create the next work branch without losing code.
branch.comment: This workflow is for short-lived task branches off the working branch. It is not the promotion path between environment branches.

promote.purpose: Explicitly merge the current source branch into the target environment branch with verification gates, safety snapshots, and a mandatory merge deletion audit. Promotion is a merge workflow, not a deploy workflow.

staging.description: Promote the current development candidate into staging as the next field-testing candidate.
staging.defaultSourceSyncCommitMessage: chore(promote): sync development before staging promotion
postPromotionValidation: Confirm origin/staging now contains the promoted staging candidate.
Confirm the staging environment can pick up the updated staging branch state.
Confirm reviewers have the intended field-test candidate and URL.
staging.comment: Use when the current development candidate should become the field-testing staging candidate.

production.description: Promote the current staging candidate into main as the next production release candidate.
postPromotionValidation: Confirm local main now contains the promoted production candidate from staging.
Confirm main is ready for SHIP from a clean local branch.
Confirm no push or deployment occurred as part of promote production.
production.comment: Use when the current staging candidate should become the next production release candidate on main, but keep SHIP and production deploy as separate explicit workflows.

promote.comment: Promotion is explicit. Use promote staging for development-to-staging and promote production for staging-to-main. Promotion is a merge workflow, not a deploy workflow. When branchModel.strategy is 'simple', promote is a no-op.

deploy.purpose: Deploy the current environment branch to its mapped runtime environment. Deployment never promotes code between branches.
promotionNotes: Use promote staging before running deploy staging.
Use promote production before running ship and deploy production.

dev.description: Deploy the development branch to the development environment.
dev.defaultPreDeployCommitMessage: chore(deploy): sync development after local verification
postDeployValidation: Confirm the development environment is serving the latest development branch build.
Confirm the intended dev URL or endpoint is reachable.
Confirm environment-aware configuration is using development values.
dev.comment: Use when local development changes need to be visible in the remote development environment for testing.

staging.description: Deploy the staging branch to the staging environment for field testing and review.
postDeployValidation: Confirm the staging environment is serving the latest staging branch build.
Confirm the staging URL or endpoint is reachable by intended reviewers.
Confirm the staging environment is using non-production configuration.
staging.comment: Staging deploy publishes already-committed staging state after verification succeeds.

production.description: Deploy the main branch to the production environment.
postDeployValidation: Confirm the production environment is serving the shipped main branch build.
Confirm the production URL or endpoint is reachable.
Confirm production configuration is active with no development or staging bindings.
production.comment: Production deploy requires an already-shipped, synced main branch.

deploy.comment: When branchModel.strategy is 'simple', only the production target is available. Dev and staging targets require 'staged' strategy.

mergeDeletionAudit.stopMessage: This merge deletes {files} files ({lines} lines). Type 'confirm deletions' to proceed.
mergeDeletionAudit.hardStopMessage: This looks like a destructive tree reset, not a normal merge. Review carefully before proceeding.

prePushNetDeletionCheck.message: This push has a net deletion of {net} lines ({added} added, {removed} removed). Confirm this is intentional.

codeLossPrevention.comment: Code-loss prevention gates apply to branch closeout merges, promote merges, and any agent-performed merge into a default or environment branch.

docsInfraPolicy.comment: For docs/infra-only changes: skip heavy verification, keep editor diagnostics and config validation.

documentation.comment: Also review any design, operational, or rollout document directly affected by the change.

runtimeAdvisory.comment: Check whether configured dev server listeners are active before handover. Report-only; never stops processes automatically.

scaffoldTemplates.comment: Template files use {{PLACEHOLDER}} syntax for values provided during the scaffold interview.
